title: ?author= filter is non-functional — uses a body extractor, not a query param
evidence: src/lib.rs:131-134 get_books(State, author: Option<String>). In axum 0.7 `String`/`Option<String>` is a request-BODY extractor, not `Query<..>`; the `?author=` query string is never read. GET /books?author=X returns ALL books unfiltered. Confirmed by cargo build (compiles) + reading the handler.
suggestion: Extract the filter via `Query(params): Query<HashMap<String,String>>` or a typed `Query<AuthorFilter>` and use params.get("author").

title: Only 1 test present; task requires at least 3, and it exercises no endpoint
evidence: src/lib.rs:328 only #[tokio::test] test_book_struct — a pure struct field-assignment test. tests/ directory is empty. `cargo test` = 1 passed. No handler/route/HTTP behavior is tested. Also violates the neutral prompt ('include tests that demonstrate the implementation meets the requirements').
suggestion: Add integration tests using axum's `oneshot`/`tower::ServiceExt` to hit POST/GET/PUT/DELETE/health and the ?author= filter (>= 3 tests).

title: PUT clobbers unspecified fields with defaults — partial update corrupts data
evidence: src/lib.rs:232-235 uses payload.title.unwrap_or_default()/year.unwrap_or(0) etc., then UPDATE sets ALL columns. A partial PUT (e.g. the README:67-76 example with only title+year) overwrites author→"", isbn→"". UpdateBookRequest's all-Option fields imply partial update but the impl ignores that.
suggestion: Fetch the existing row and only overwrite fields that are Some(..), or build a dynamic UPDATE for provided fields.

title: SQLite is in-memory only — no persistence across restarts
evidence: src/lib.rs:66 SqlitePool::connect("sqlite::memory:"). REQUIREMENTS R7 how_to_verify: 'Persistence uses SQLite/embedded DB, not just in-memory state.' Data is lost on every restart; README:98 acknowledges 'in-memory database'.
suggestion: Connect to a file-backed SQLite URL (e.g. sqlite://books.db) so data persists.

title: Entire application in a single 342-line lib.rs
evidence: src/lib.rs holds types, DB setup, all six handlers, and the router (maintainability=0.2583 in scores.json). No module separation (models/db/handlers).
suggestion: Split into modules (models, db, handlers, routes) for readability; optional for this task size.
